summary:
One unit of PRBCs generally increases hematocrit by 3-5%. This increase is based on standard clinical guidelines and varies slightly depending on patient factors.  

Note that this answer assumes “HCV” was intended as an intentional typo for “HCT,” since no medical condition called HCV relates directly to hematocrit changes from transfusion.

We are asked only to summarize Agent1’s *reasoning*, not restate facts or add commentary beyond what he said.

Final summary:

One unit of PRBCs increases hematocrit by approximately 3–5% according to established clinical literature. The variation arises from individual physiological differences such as body composition and pre-transfusion hemoglobin levels.
